content:
3 min read
Just now

--

For the past year, I’ve been quietly obsessed with NotebookLM. If you haven’t used it yet, it’s a tool that allows you to upload massive amounts of source material and interact with it directly, ensuring every answer is strictly grounded in the documents you provided. As someone whose life is deeply rooted in a relationship with Jesus, my mind immediately went to the single most important library of texts in my life: the Bible.
I wanted a tool that could help me see the grand narrative of Scripture without the fluff. I wanted something that wouldn’t just give me comfortable, predictable answers, but would actually challenge my assumptions and push me deeper into the Word.
So, I built it. And today, I’m opening it up to the public.
What is This Notebook?
I uploaded all 66 books of the Holy Bible using the New International Version (NIV) text into a single, comprehensive NotebookLM workspace.
But the real magic isn’t just having the text there — it’s how the workspace is trained to talk back to you. I engineered this specific notebook to act as an accessible biblical synthesizer and a critical, down-to-earth peer.
👉 Click here to access the 66-Book NIV Bible Study Notebook (Note: You will need a free Google account to open and use the link).
Why This Approach Changes Everything
Most AI tools or generic commentaries tend to tell you what you want to hear, or they rely heavily on a few famous, comfortable verses. This notebook operates on a completely different philosophy, designed to be uniquely trusted, grounded, and rigorous:

• Genesis-to-Revelation Synthesis: If you ask a question about a topic like justice, suffering, or covenant, the notebook won’t just look at one verse. It is designed to pull from at least three different sections of the Bible (like the Law, the Prophets, and the Letters) to give you a complete, multi-book view.

• 100% Grounded in Text: It completely skips the academic jargon and complex theological phrasing. Instead, it speaks in everyday language but backs up every single point with direct scripture citations and quotes. If it makes a claim, it shows you the exact chapter and verse.

• Embracing the Beautiful Tensions: The Bible isn’t a flat document; it holds different angles. This notebook actively highlights those nuances — contrasting the practical, everyday wisdom of Proverbs with the honest, existential struggles of Ecclesiastes, giving you a bigger, richer picture.

• Loving, Truthful Pushback: This is my favorite part. The notebook doesn’t “glaze” or offer empty compliments. Its first instinct is to stress-test your ideas. If you bring a weak assumption or a theological blind spot to the text, it will gently but directly challenge you with scripture, forcing you to earn your conclusions.
Grounded in Faith, Driven by Truth
My faith in Jesus isn’t just a part of my life but the foundation of it. Because I love the Lord, I want to know His Word deeply, honestly, and without filters, but also synthesizing that with modern tools. I don’t want an AI that just nods along with my half-baked thoughts — I want to be sharpened. As Proverbs 27:17 says, “As iron sharpens iron, so one person sharpens another.”
Whether you are preparing a sermon, diving into personal devotions, or wrestling with hard questions about the text, I hope this resource serves you as a helpful, rigorous companion on your journey.
Dive in, ask the hard questions, let the text challenge you, and let’s grow closer to the truth together.
